violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Per PIC, application was sent for a large part of the school district last month. No applications on file. PIC stated they will resubmit. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

Various foods were observed held in the reach-in refrigerator above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was able to reach 41 degrees after closed for an extended period of time. Lunch ended for the day so food was able to stay in unit to reach a temperature of 41 degrees or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The reach-in refrigerator was in poor repair. Unit was unable to recover temperature in a timely manner to ensure food maintained an adequate temperature. Per PIC, unit is getting replaced tomorrow morning. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food has been in unit since at least yesterday. Food was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Reach in refrigerator door gasket was observed in poor repair. The reach-in unit was observed in poor repair. Items below the top shelf were observed with elevated temperatures. Unit to be serviced. No TCS foods shall be stored below top shelf until serviced. Employees to store foods in a manner that does not block air flow. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Salad and chopped fruit were observed held at 46-51 degrees Fahrenheit in the salad bar. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food was placed in the salad bar about 20 minutes prior to inspection. Pans of food were not in contact with the ice packs stored underneath them during time of inspection. The person in charge put the ice packs up higher so they made contact with the pans of food during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023
